behaviors.description: The speech-to-text output preserves speaker labels so that the summary can attribute statements to specific attendees.
behaviors.justification: Failure depends on real speech-model output quality (speaker diarization/label preservation), so a stubbed transcript would hide real misses (LLM-DEP + MOCK-HIDE). Real network/API is involved, but the AI-output boundary is the stronger minimum, so Agentic over System. Rejected Workflow because behavior text focuses on STT label preservation itself, not multiple model decisions in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Run real audio with multiple speakers through the production STT API, then assert transcript chunks preserve correct speaker labels needed for attribution downstream.

behaviors.description: Transcript parsing extracts explicit task assignments (assignee + task text) into the summary input structure.
behaviors.justification: This behavior is parser logic on transcript text to structured fields; no required OS/network/subprocess or model judgment in the behavior wording (MOCK-MISS points to local logic). Rejected Integration because no explicit cross-module handoff/state propagation requirement is stated.
behaviors.test_description: Feed transcript text fixtures into the parsing function/object and assert extracted assignee and task text fields match expected structured output.

behaviors.description: The live summary output contains at least one action item for every attendee who was assigned a task during the meeting.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real LLM generation decisions (coverage of each assigned attendee), which deterministic fixtures cannot validate (LLM-DEP + deterministic-assertion trap). Rejected Workflow because this can be tested by providing a prepared transcript with known assignments and validating one live summarization step, without requiring multiple agentic stages.
behaviors.test_description: Send a transcript containing explicit assignments for multiple attendees to the real summarization model and assert the produced action-items list includes at least one item per assigned attendee.

behaviors.description: Decision extraction logic routes identified decisions into the `decisions` section schema rather than generic narrative fields.
behaviors.justification: Behavior text targets routing logic after decisions are already identified; that is local transformation/schema mapping logic. No required real infra or real model output for this specific failure boundary. Rejected Integration because no explicit module-boundary failure is required by wording.
behaviors.test_description: Call the decision-routing function with fixtures of pre-identified decision objects and assert output populates `decisions` schema fields and not narrative fields.

behaviors.description: Key decisions mentioned in the transcript appear in the live summary's `decisions` section, not buried in the narrative.
behaviors.justification: This validates model output content placement/quality in a live summary, so mocks would mask real extraction/placement failures (LLM-DEP + MOCK-HIDE). Rejected Workflow because behavior can be validated in a single real summarization pass over a transcript; multi-agentic sequencing is not required by the behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Submit a transcript with clearly stated decisions to the real summarizer and assert those decisions are present in the returned `decisions` section rather than only narrative text.

behaviors.description: When the audio file is longer than 60 minutes, the service splits it into segments and processes them sequentially, merging the results into a single coherent summary.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires sequential multi-step processing across segments plus final merged summary coherence; this chains multiple agentic calls (STT/summary across segments and merge outcome) and orchestration correctness (MULTI-STEP). Integration-only tests with stubs can check order/calls but miss coherence failures caused by real model outputs.
behaviors.test_description: Run a real >60-minute recording through production segmentation flow, verify segment-by-segment processing order, then assert final merged summary remains coherent and structurally complete across segment boundaries.

behaviors.description: The full pipeline (upload audio, transcribe, summarize, deliver structured output) completes without manual intervention for a real meeting recording.
behaviors.justification: This is an end-to-end pipeline with real upload/transcription/summarization/output delivery and multiple dependent agentic stages; failures arise from stage handoffs and cumulative model behavior (REAL-INFRA + LLM-DEP + MULTI-STEP). Rejected lower levels because any mocked stage can hide real cross-stage and model-output failures implied by 'real meeting recording' and 'full pipeline'.
behaviors.test_description: Execute an end-to-end run on a real meeting audio file through production interfaces and assert successful completion, valid structured output schema, and no human intervention at any stage.
